branchName: ralph/<story-id>-<slug> (per-story branch prefix — see agents/ralph.md; each story gets its own branch off main, not one shared branch)
description: Single-page scroll portfolio (Svelte 5 + SvelteKit, adapter-static, Tailwind CSS) with a 'terminal/CRT meets botanical' design language — hard edges, offset hard-shadows, green/purple grass-and-wildflower palette, distinctive no-AI-glow animations. Built per docs/00-overview.md through docs/08-build-plan.md. Delivered one story at a time: each story branches off main, opens a PR, and waits for human merge before the next story starts.

userStories.title: Scaffold SvelteKit project with static adapter and tooling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working SvelteKit + TypeScript project scaffolded in this (currently empty) repo, configured for static prerendering and with baseline tooling in place, so all later work has a stable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: SvelteKit project initialized with TypeScript in the repo root

@sveltejs/adapter-static installed and configured (in svelte.config.js, or inline via the sveltekit() Vite plugin if this SvelteKit version has no separate svelte.config.js); root layout sets export const prerender = true
Tailwind CSS installed and wired into the Vite/SvelteKit build (a Tailwind utility class renders correctly on a placeholder page)
ESLint + Prettier (with prettier-plugin-tailwindcss) configured; svelte-check available as the typecheck command
npm run build produces a static build/ output
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Implement design tokens via Tailwind v4 @theme and load fonts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the palette and typography tokens from docs/01-design-system.md expressed as a Tailwind v4 @theme block (this project's Tailwind is CSS-first, not tailwind.config.ts) so every later component styles against one consistent source, with both fonts self-hosted and loaded.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/layout.css defines an @theme block with colors (base #111a10, surface #182415, green #5fa842/bright #84cc4f, purple #9b6dc4/deep #6b4a8f, text #eaf0e4/muted #b9c2b3) and fontFamily tokens (font-mono: JetBrains Mono stack, font-sans: Inter stack)
Tailwind v4 auto-emits these as real :root, :host CSS custom properties in the compiled build output (verified by inspecting build output CSS) — no separate hand-written mirrored :root block needed
JetBrains Mono and Inter are self-hosted (e.g. via @fontsource-variable packages) and loaded with font-display: swap
A test page renders base background, text color, and both fonts correctly with no layout shift after font load
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add projects and certs data models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed data files for projects and cybersecurity content so sections can be built against a stable content contract before real copy exists.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/data/projects.ts exports ProjectImage and Project interfaces (per docs/04-software-section.md: slug, title, summary, description?, techStack, images, links {demo?, repo?, writeup?}, year required) and a projects: Project[] array with 2 seed/placeholder entries

src/lib/data/certs.ts exports CertStatus, Certification, Accomplishment, SecurityTool interfaces (per docs/05-cybersecurity-section.md, including required category on SecurityTool) and seed arrays for certifications, accomplishments, securityTools
All seed entries typecheck against their interfaces with no 'any'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add hero, about, and contact data models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed data files for the Hero, About (including Experience), and contact-form endpoint config so those sections and the footer are content-driven from a single edit point.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/data/hero.ts exports HeroContent interface (bootLines, name, title, tagline, resumeUrl, githubUrl, linkedinUrl) and a hero: HeroContent value with placeholder resume/GitHub/LinkedIn URLs

src/lib/data/about.ts exports ExperienceEntry and AboutContent interfaces (narrative, currentlyBuilding, experience) and an about: AboutContent value including 2 placeholder Experience entries (Finback 670, InvitaHealth)
src/lib/data/contact.ts exports a config value holding the placeholder form-submit endpoint (swappable later for Resend) with no other logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build page shell with four section placeholders
userStories.description: As a visitor, I need the single-page scroll structure in place (Hero, About, Software, Cybersecurity as distinct sections with stable ids) so navigation and later section content have something to attach to.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/+page.svelte composes four section elements with ids #hero, #about, #software, #cybersecurity in that order
Each section is a distinct Svelte component file (HeroSection.svelte, AboutSection.svelte, SoftwareSection.svelte, CybersecuritySection.svelte) even if currently rendering placeholder content
Page scrolls continuously through all four sections with no overlap or broken layout at mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints (640px/1024px)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney (Chrome CLI automation): all four sections exist/visible, getBoundingClientRect() confirms they stack at 0/800/1600/2400/3200px with zero overlap or gap, screenshots confirm correct rendering at desktop and mobile. First verification pass incorrectly assumed no browser tool was available and used curl-only structural checks instead; corrected in a follow-up commit on this branch and in the PR comments. Full detail in agents/progress.txt's US-005 entry.

userStories.title: Implement sticky Nav with IntersectionObserver active-section logic
userStories.description: As a visitor, I need a sticky nav that highlights whichever section I'm currently viewing and lets me jump to any section, so I can orient and navigate the single-page site.
acceptanceCriteria: Nav.svelte is sticky/fixed at the top across all breakpoints, using Svelte 5 runes ($state for activeSection, $effect to set up/tear down the observer)

A single IntersectionObserver with rootMargin '-45% 0px -45% 0px' (or equivalent center-crossing threshold) drives activeSection; exactly one section is active at a time with no flicker during continuous scroll

Nav shows a home/logo item (acts as scroll-to-top / back-to-top) plus About, Software, Cybersecurity links; clicking a link smooth-scrolls to that section and updates location.hash without a full reload
Nav is transparent/borderless while within Hero and gains a background + border once scrolled past Hero (two-state behavior)
On mobile, nav collapses to a hamburger-triggered full-screen overlay menu (not a condensed bar)
Active nav item is indicated by both a color change and a non-color marker (e.g. bracket or underline), not color alone
All nav items are keyboard-focusable with visible focus-visible states
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: nav renders transparent at top of Hero and docks (border + surface bg) once scrolled; clicking a link smooth-scrolls, updates the hash, and highlights the active link via both color and a '>' prefix marker; logo click returns to top; mobile hamburger opens a full-screen terminal-style overlay with body scroll lock (confirmed via aria-expanded + body.style.overflow, since responsive-width click testing via CLI viewport override was flaky between separate invocations — screenshots at 375px confirmed the responsive class split visually). All nav items are keyboard-focusable.

userStories.title: Build Hero section with CRT boot-sequence and typed-text effect
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the Hero to open with a distinctive terminal boot animation before the name/title/CTAs resolve, per the site's WOW-factor design direction, so the first impression is memorable and on-theme.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroSection.svelte reads content from data/hero.ts (bootLines, name, title, tagline, resumeUrl, githubUrl, linkedinUrl)
A brief (~600-900ms) simulated terminal boot sequence types out 2-3 lines before the final name/title/tagline appears, using $state for line/character progress driven by an $effect timer
Final hero text is present in the DOM immediately and readable by screen readers regardless of animation timing (not solely revealed via animation)
prefers-reduced-motion: reduce skips directly to the fully-typed final state with no animation delay
CTA row renders resume, GitHub, LinkedIn links (placeholder URLs) plus a 'View Projects' control that scrolls to #software
Hero content is fully visible without scrolling at 375x667 and 1440x900 viewports
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: boot lines type correctly (no runaway re-render bug, fixed during this story — see progress.txt), Hero content (name/title/tagline/CTAs) fades in after ~800ms boot sequence, all present in DOM immediately with correct text content, all CTAs keyboard-focusable, View Projects scrolls to #software. prefers-reduced-motion path verified by code review only (rodney has no CLI flag to emulate that media feature).

userStories.title: Build About section with narrative, currently-building line, and Experience list
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read a short narrative about the site owner, what they're currently building, and their work experience, so I understand their background beyond the Hero tagline.
acceptanceCriteria: AboutSection.svelte reads narrative paragraphs and currentlyBuilding from data/about.ts and renders them as body copy in the Inter font
A 'currently building' line is displayed in monospace styling (e.g. '$ currently building: ...')
ExperienceList.svelte renders about.experience as a compact timeline (role, org, date range) with 2 placeholder entries (Finback 670, InvitaHealth)
No portrait/avatar image is rendered or reserved as empty space (none exists yet)
Section reflows correctly from single-column (mobile) to any wider layout (desktop) with no overlap
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: two-column layout (narrative+currently-building / Experience) at desktop, reflows to single-column stacked at 375px with no overlap; both Experience entries render with formatted Mon YYYY date ranges (including 'Present' for the open-ended InvitaHealth entry); no avatar/image present or space reserved for one.

userStories.title: Build Software section project grid wired to data
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see the owner's software projects as cards in a responsive grid, so I can scan their work at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectCard.svelte accepts a project: Project prop and renders thumbnail (first image), title, summary, tech-stack tags, year, and a distinct 'Live Demo' link/button when links.demo is present
SoftwareSection.svelte renders one ProjectCard per entry in data/projects.ts in array order, in a grid that reflows 1-up mobile / 2-up tablet / 3-up desktop
Adding a throwaway 3rd seed project to projects.ts requires no .svelte file changes to render correctly
Card hover state uses the design system's translate + growing-shadow 'lift' interaction, not a generic color-only hover
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: 3-up grid at 1440px (added/removed a throwaway 3rd project to confirm no component changes needed, including Live Demo/Repo links appearing correctly when links are present); compiled CSS confirms sm:grid-cols-2 at >=640px and lg:grid-cols-3 at >=1024px (grid-cols-1 base). Tablet/mobile live-scroll screenshots were unreliable due to a known rodney viewport-emulation quirk (documented in progress.txt) — verified via compiled-CSS breakpoint inspection instead. Copilot review was unavailable (quota limit) on this PR; manual self-review caught that every image had loading="lazy" including the first card, violating NFR-1 (first visible thumbnail should be eager) — fixed with a priority prop from the each-block index, confirmed via rodney that only the first card is eager.

userStories.title: Build accessible image lightbox with keyboard nav and touch swipe
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to click a project's thumbnail to preview its full image gallery without leaving the page, and navigate it with keyboard or touch, so previewing screenshots feels fast and doesn't require external links.
acceptanceCriteria: Lightbox.svelte accepts images, activeIndex, onClose, onNavigate props; SoftwareSection.svelte owns a single activeProjectSlug/activeImageIndex $state controlling one shared lightbox instance (not one per card)
Clicking a project thumbnail opens the lightbox to that project's images; Escape closes it, ArrowLeft/ArrowRight navigate, on-screen prev/next controls also work
Touch/swipe gesture navigates images on touch devices in addition to buttons/keyboard
Focus is trapped inside the lightbox while open and restored to the triggering thumbnail on close
Background scroll is locked while open without introducing horizontal layout shift (scrollbar-width compensated)
Projects with images.length <= 1 hide the gallery-open affordance rather than opening a dead-control lightbox
All images have non-empty, meaningful alt text sourced from the data model
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney (temporarily gave CivSail 2 images to exercise the gallery, reverted after): gallery-open button hidden for both seed projects (1 image each); with 2 images, clicking the thumbnail opens the lightbox with focus on the dialog; ArrowRight/ArrowLeft navigate and wrap around; Escape closes and restores focus to the triggering button; backdrop click (elementFromPoint at a corner) closes, clicking the image itself does not; Tab from the last focusable element wraps to the first (focus trap); touch swipe left/right (via real Touch() objects, not plain object literals) navigates next/prev; body.style.overflow is 'hidden' while open and restored to '' on close. Copilot review was unavailable (quota limit) again; manual self-review caught the dialog being nested inside <main> instead of a true top-level overlay (Tab-trap alone doesn't stop AT browse-mode navigation into background content) — fixed with a portal action (src/lib/actions/portal.ts) moving the dialog to a direct child of <body>, plus an inert-toggling effect on its siblings. That fix introduced a second bug (focus-restore racing inert-removal cleanup order across two separate effects, silently failing), caught by re-verifying with rodney and fixed by merging both concerns into one effect with a deterministic cleanup order.

userStories.title: Add build-time image optimization for project images
userStories.description: As a developer, I need project images served as responsive, modern-format assets built at compile time, so the site stays fast without a runtime image service.
acceptanceCriteria: @sveltejs/enhanced-img is installed and wired into the Vite config
Project thumbnail and lightbox images use enhanced-img (or its <enhanced:img> equivalent) producing responsive srcset/sizes with AVIF/WebP + fallback
All images except the first visible card's thumbnail use loading="lazy"
Build succeeds with optimized image output present in build/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: @sveltejs/enhanced-img wired via a custom import.meta.glob lookup (src/lib/images.ts) rather than static per-file imports, since project images are referenced dynamically via string keys in projects.ts. Verified in compiled build output: real AVIF/WebP/JPEG variants generated at multiple resolutions, <picture> markup with srcset/sizes present in build/index.html, width/height auto-set (prevents CLS), and the US-009 eager/lazy loading split on the first card is preserved. Confirmed visually with rodney that rendering is unchanged from before optimization.

userStories.title: Build Cybersecurity section cert cards with status states
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see the owner's certifications with clear active/expiring-soon/in-progress status, so I can gauge their current credentials at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: CertCard.svelte accepts a cert: Certification prop and renders name, issuer, formatted date(s), a verify link when verifyUrl is present, and renders correctly when optional fields (logo, verifyUrl, expiryDate, credentialId) are absent

A cert with status 'active' and an expiryDate within 90 days renders a visually distinct 'expiring soon' badge (computed at render time, not stored), separate from plain 'active' and from 'in-progress' styling
CybersecuritySection.svelte renders one CertCard per entry in data/certs.ts's certifications array, visually distinct from the Software section's card treatment while using the same design tokens
Verify links open in a new tab with rel="noopener noreferrer"
Adding a throwaway test cert to certs.ts requires no .svelte file changes to render correctly
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: both seed active certs correctly show 'Expiring Soon' (their 2026-09-01 expiry is within 90 days of the current date); CPTS shows 'In Progress' with a dashed border. Temporarily added throwaway certs to exercise 'Active', 'Expired', and verify-link states (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer confirmed), plus a stale-active cert (status still 'active' but expiryDate already past) — self-review caught this would have silently rendered as plain 'Active' with no warning, fixed by deriving 'Expired' from expiryDate regardless of the stored status. All throwaway certs reverted before commit. Visually distinct from Software section (2-col flat-bordered list vs. 3-col hover-lift cards), same design tokens.

userStories.title: Build Accomplishments list and grouped Tools display
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see the owner's CTF/competition accomplishments and their security tool proficiency grouped by category, so I understand their practical security experience beyond formal certs.
acceptanceCriteria: Accomplishments.svelte accepts items: Accomplishment[] and renders them as a list/timeline distinct from the cert-card grid treatment
ToolsList.svelte accepts tools: SecurityTool[] and renders them as tag/pill chips grouped by their category field (e.g. a heading per category with its tools beneath)
Both components are rendered inside CybersecuritySection.svelte, sourced from data/certs.ts's accomplishments and securityTools arrays
Rendering with seed/placeholder data produces no broken layout for empty or single-item groups
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: Accomplishments renders both seed entries with the '>' marker; ToolsList groups the 2 seed tools into 2 distinct single-item category groups (Offensive, Defensive) with no broken layout. Temporarily added a throwaway accomplishment exercising description/date/link (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer confirmed) and reverted. Self-review made the decorative '>' marker aria-hidden for consistency with the same pattern in Nav.svelte. Both sections conditionally render only if their array is non-empty.

userStories.title: Build contact form and footer with placeholder submit backend
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a way to contact the site owner via a form, so that I don't need to already know their email. As the developer, I need the form's backend to be a placeholder today and a one-line config swap to Resend later.
acceptanceCriteria: ContactForm.svelte holds $state for field values and submit status (idle/submitting/success/error), with all fields keyboard-operable and labeled

Submitting the form calls the endpoint defined in data/contact.ts (not hardcoded inline) and shows a success state without requiring a runtime server, consistent with the static adapter-static deploy target
Footer.svelte renders ContactForm plus the placeholder resume/GitHub/LinkedIn links from data/hero.ts
Swapping the endpoint value in data/contact.ts is the only change needed to point the form at a different backend
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: full submit flow works end-to-end (fill fields, submit, success message with entered name shown). Key finding: fetch(contact.submitUrl) in normal cors mode rejects ('Failed to fetch') against the current placeholder URL since it's not a real CORS-aware backend; using mode:'no-cors' resolves successfully (opaque response) as long as the network layer can reach the host, which is what lets the form show genuine success today with zero server, per docs/00-overview.md's now-resolved open question. All fields properly labeled (labels[0] confirmed), keyboard-focusable, native required validation blocks empty submission (checkValidity() false). Footer resume/GitHub/LinkedIn links confirmed correct (GitHub/LinkedIn open in new tab with proper rel, Resume does not).

userStories.title: Add scanline overlay and glitch-on-hover signature interactions
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the site to feel distinctively CRT/terminal-themed through subtle ambient motion and a glitch effect on interactive headings, so the first impression stands out from generic AI-portfolio aesthetics.
acceptanceCriteria: ScanlineOverlay.svelte renders a fixed-position, pointer-events:none, aria-hidden, low-opacity repeating-gradient scanline texture with slow drift animation, layered above the base background
Section headings and nav items glitch (brief RGB-channel-split / character-jitter, ~150ms) on hover using --color-green-bright / --color-purple channel offsets
Neither effect uses blur, glow, gradients-as-fill, or backdrop-filter (glassmorphism) — verified by inspecting the applied CSS
Both effects are disabled under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce (scanline stops drifting or is hidden; glitch hover reduces to a plain color change)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: scanline overlay confirmed aria-hidden=true, pointer-events:none, position:fixed, opacity:0.04, filter/backdrop-filter both none (no blur/glassmorphism); real .hover() on nav button/section headings triggers the glitch-flicker animation (confirmed via getComputedStyle().animationName and by reading the compiled stylesheet rule directly, which shows a one-shot 150ms animation with text-shadow values that are all hard 2-value offsets, zero blur radius). Both signature interactions' prefers-reduced-motion overrides confirmed present in compiled build CSS (rodney can't emulate that media feature directly, consistent with the US-007 limitation).

userStories.title: Add scroll-reveal action and growth-accent motif across all sections
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want section content to reveal smoothly as I scroll to it, with a small botanical motif accenting transitions, so scrolling through the page feels intentional rather than static.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/actions/reveal.ts is a Svelte action wrapping an IntersectionObserver that adds an is-visible class (opacity/transform only, no layout-affecting properties) whenever an element crosses ~20% into the viewport, and removes it when the element leaves — repeatable (re-triggers every time the element re-enters view, per owner feedback superseding the original one-shot plan), not a one-time trigger
Grid items (project cards, cert cards) stagger reveal by ~60ms per item; standalone headings reveal individually with no stagger
A small recurring botanical accent (animated SVG or ASCII-style glyph) appears at least at section transitions
prefers-reduced-motion: reduce shows all reveal-target elements immediately with no animated transition
No cumulative layout shift is introduced by reveal animations (verified via DevTools CLS or Lighthouse)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Verified with rodney: reveal elements start with class='reveal' (opacity:0) before scrolling into view, gain 'is-visible' (opacity:1) once ~20% intersecting. Owner feedback after initial review: wanted the reveal to replay every time an element re-enters view rather than only once per page load — updated reveal.ts to remove 'is-visible' on exit (IntersectionObserver stays attached, never unobserves) instead of unobserving after the first trigger; re-verified scroll-away-then-back re-adds 'is-visible' correctly. Staggered grid items (ProjectCard/CertCard wrapped in use:reveal={{delayMs: i*60}} divs) both eventually reveal correctly. GrowthAccent (4 instances, one per section transition) confirmed present, undrawn (stroke-dashoffset:60px) before reveal and drawn (0px) after, no filter/blur. Both reduced-motion overrides (.reveal, .growth-accent path) confirmed in compiled build CSS. Deliberately did NOT apply the generic reveal action to Hero's heading, since it already has its own dedicated boot-sequence-driven opacity transition — layering the generic scroll-reveal on top would create two competing opacity systems on the same element.

userStories.title: Set up CI with typecheck/lint/build gate and deploy to default subdomain
userStories.description: As a developer, I need automated CI that blocks broken code from merging and a working deploy pipeline to a live URL, so the site can ship reliably without manual steps.
acceptanceCriteria: A GitHub Actions workflow runs typecheck, lint, and build on every pull request and fails the check if any step fails
A separate deploy step publishes the build/ output to the platform's default subdomain (GitHub Pages or Netlify, not yet the custom domain — DNS is handled separately by the owner) on push to main
The live default-subdomain URL serves the site correctly over HTTPS after a push to main
Lighthouse Performance/Accessibility/Best Practices scores on the live URL each meet or exceed 95
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented .github/workflows/ci.yml (check/lint/build on every PR + push to main) and deploy.yml (builds with BASE_PATH=/<repo-name>, deploys via actions/upload-pages-artifact + actions/deploy-pages on push to main). REQUIRES a one-time manual repo setting change before deploy.yml can succeed: Settings -> Pages -> Source -> 'GitHub Actions' (a dashboard-only change, can't be done via commit). Live-URL and Lighthouse acceptance criteria can only be verified after merge + that setting change + an actual push to main triggers the real deploy -- not verifiable from this session. Verified everything that CAN be checked locally: build succeeds both with and without BASE_PATH set (simulating the exact deploy-workflow environment); found and fixed a real bug this surfaced -- hero.resumeUrl ('/resume.pdf') was hardcoded absolute and broke the entire prerendered build under a base path (SvelteKit's internal-link check failed with a 404), fixed by prefixing with $app/paths's base in HeroSection.svelte and Footer.svelte. Confirmed post-fix: build succeeds under BASE_PATH, resume link resolves correctly, enhanced-img asset paths and JS entry points all correctly prefixed.

userStories.title: Fill in placeholder-realistic content across all data files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need every data file populated with fuller, plausible-looking content using the owner's real project/employer/cert names (CivSail, Nexus, Finback 670, InvitaHealth, CPTS) instead of minimal seed stubs, so the deployed site looks complete while every invented fact stays clearly marked for later replacement with the owner's real copy.
acceptanceCriteria: data/projects.ts has two full entries: CivSail and Nexus, each with a plausible summary/description, a realistic techStack array, at least 2 placeholder images with meaningful alt text, a links.demo and links.repo placeholder URL, and a year

data/certs.ts certifications array has 2 entries with status 'active' and an expiryDate within 90 days (to exercise the 'expiring soon' badge) plus a 3rd entry named CPTS with status 'in-progress' and no expiryDate
data/certs.ts accomplishments array has at least 2 plausible CTF/competition-placement entries (title, description, date)
data/certs.ts securityTools array has entries spanning at least 3 categories (e.g. Offensive, Defensive, Forensics/GRC)
data/about.ts experience array has 2 entries: Finback 670 and InvitaHealth, each with a role, org, and a plausible startDate/endDate (or omitted endDate if treated as current)
Every invented (non-owner-confirmed) fact — dates, descriptions, placements, tool categorization — is marked with a PLACEHOLDER_COPY comment/sentinel so it is grep-able for later replacement
No empty states, Lorem ipsum, or broken layout results from the fuller data anywhere in the app
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
